updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: Good volatility today in bursts. 7482 recovery was the long trigger. Targets were 7492, 7506, 7511, 7521, then 7533/40 to backtest the overnight breakdown shelf. Massive success for bulls if this recovers. Newsletter out soon with next entries
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7482,7492,7506,7511,7521,7533,7540) are trade recap/observations or already in morning plan; no new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text:
Summer trading slow but we get 1 volatility spikes daily to give us longs in #ES_F. 7482 reclaim was last entry. Targets I gave ystd were 7492, 7506, 7511, 7521. 7540 was my final target & magnet. We are here.

Plan: Ride runner. 7547, 7565 next. Watch 21 entry from newsletter https://t.co/MhiD4iXJ9R
updates.notes: All mentioned prices (7482, 7506, 7521, 7547, 7565) are morning-plan levels or prior-day targets; 7540 is a hindsight magnet ref; no new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @fiveinchhammer Yes, I wouldn't touch it now personally but not every entry I give here is something I'd trade. I only trade the high quality newsletter Failed Breakdowns. No shorts, Level Reclaims, Level Buys or anything else for me
updates.notes: No prices mentioned at all; tweet is a personal trading philosophy reply with zero actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @fiveinchhammer Few people have the patience I do though to wait and most don't care about having a 95%+ win rate either so I give other entries also
updates.notes: No price levels mentioned at all; purely conversational reply about trading patience and win rate.

updates.tweet_text: @saltyairlife There is definitely a large bull flag there. I never assume any type of downside in ES though, I am always bullish in an uptrend (in an uptrend you should be a permabull and never even think of downside). If it fails we would see an elevator down sell though
updates.notes: Conversational reply about bull flag pattern and trend bias; no specific actionable price level named.

updates.tweet_text:
Summer trading is very easy and we've done the same thing daily for weeks. 1) Wait for elevator down sell 2) Take the Failed Breakdown 3) Ride runner for 1-2 days as we grind up. Last entry was 7482 recovery. My target was 7540 (hit).

Today's targets 7547 (hit), 7565, 7577 next https://t.co/y60QH1QkzB
updates.notes: 7577 named explicitly as 'next' target beyond 7565 — not in morning plan. 7547/7565 are morning-plan reaffirmations. 7540 is hindsight.

updates.tweet_text: @TruthTellingX Then you don't enter a trade. The only triggers if it is a Failed Breakdown. Failed Breakdowns work best in downtrends and bear markets
updates.notes: Reply explaining FBD mechanics conceptually; no price named, no actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @MizuTraderr Summer trading is usually easy and low volatility. ES has fairly regular seasonal volatility patterns
updates.notes: Pure seasonal commentary, no price named, no actionable level of any kind.
Tweet is a conversational reply listing months/dates with no price levels mentioned at all.

updates.tweet_text: @MizuTraderr High volatility means more setups, but if you don't know what your doing it can also means more losses. Markets like this are just easy as long as you don't overtrade and wait for the elevator down sells
updates.notes: Pure commentary on trading volatility and strategy; no price named, no actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text:
One way traffic and free money today in #ES_F. As warned all day, just let that runner pay. Now +100 from yesterday's 7482 Reclaim Long. 8am targets given were 7547 (hit), 7565 (hit), 7577 (hit), 7597 final (en route).

Then, time for the major breakout to 7611, 7637+ https://t.co/VZqfZNDDwX

updates.tweet_text: @PX99128070 Its a reaction zone but I don't predict price paths. I just wait for elevator down sell, take the failed breakdown, hold runner. I know the range will breakout but as for when or how, that is up to price
updates.notes: Pure methodology/process commentary. No specific price levels named or actionable triggers identified.

updates.tweet_text: @Marcel22520116 It has to be a major shelf of support that breaks down to produce a large selloff. ITs very specific, has to be a multi-day very clear shelf with numerous tests that then collapses through. Can't just be any random support
updates.notes: Reply explaining a conceptual rule about shelf breakdowns; no specific price named or actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: Today's final target was 7597. Targets given at 8am were 7547, 7565, 7577, then 7597. We hit ~7597, and rejected. Now, we will rebuild structure and then prep for breakout. We are building a 1.5 week flag which is 60% mature. Newsletter out soon with entries
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7547, 7565, 7577, 7597) are morning-plan levels or hindsight references to targets already given at 8am. No new actionable levels named.
